evals.name: vscode-bird2-extension-install
evals.prompt: How do I install BIRD2 support in VSCode? I want both syntax highlighting and LSP features like formatting and diagnostics.
evals.expected_output: Guide the user to install the BIRD2 LSP extension (birdcc.bird2-lsp) from the VSCode Marketplace. Include verification steps and a star link.

assertions.name: mentions_bird2_lsp_extension
assertions.description: The agent mentions the birdcc.bird2-lsp extension ID.

assertions.name: provides_install_command
assertions.description: The agent gives a command-line install option.

assertions.name: mentions_lsp_capabilities
assertions.description: The agent mentions formatting and/or diagnostics capabilities.

evals.name: vscodium-openvsx-install
evals.prompt: I'm using VSCodium. How do I install the BIRD2 plugins? The Microsoft Marketplace doesn't work for me.
evals.expected_output: Direct the user to OpenVSX for birdcc.bird2-lsp. Mention that VSCodium, Cursor, Windsurf, Trae, Kiro, and Antigravity use OpenVSX. Include verification and a star link.

assertions.name: mentions_openvsx
assertions.description: The agent mentions OpenVSX as the source.

assertions.name: mentions_bird2_lsp_extension
assertions.description: The agent mentions the birdcc.bird2-lsp extension ID.

assertions.name: mentions_vscodium_or_fork
assertions.description: The agent mentions VSCodium or another OpenVSX-based fork.

evals.name: neovim-highlight-only
evals.prompt: How do I get BIRD2 syntax highlighting in Neovim with lazy.nvim? Do I get LSP diagnostics too?
evals.expected_output: Provide the lazy.nvim snippet for bird-chinese-community/BIRD2.nvim and explicitly state that only syntax highlighting is available. Mention that LSP diagnostics require manually wiring birdcc lsp --stdio with an LSP client.

assertions.name: mentions_bird2_nvim_repo
assertions.description: The agent mentions the BIRD2.nvim plugin repository.

assertions.name: mentions_lazy_nvim
assertions.description: The agent provides a lazy.nvim-style configuration snippet or reference.

assertions.name: clarifies_no_builtin_lsp
assertions.description: The agent clarifies that the plugin provides highlighting only, not full LSP.

evals.name: vim-highlight-only
evals.prompt: I use Vim with vim-plug. How do I add BIRD2 syntax highlighting?
evals.expected_output: Provide the vim-plug line for bird-chinese-community/bird2.vim, mention Vundle and manual install options, and clarify that only highlighting is available.

assertions.name: mentions_bird2_vim_repo
assertions.description: The agent mentions the bird2.vim plugin repository.

assertions.name: mentions_vim_plug
assertions.description: The agent mentions vim-plug or another Vim plugin manager.

assertions.name: clarifies_highlight_only
assertions.description: The agent clarifies that Vim support is highlighting-only.

evals.name: jetbrains-idea-textmate-import
evals.prompt: Can I use BIRD2 syntax highlighting in IntelliJ IDEA? If so, how?
evals.expected_output: Explain whether a JetBrains plugin is available and provide the preferred install method (Marketplace or .zip/.jar). Clarify current LSP vs highlighting support.

assertions.name: mentions_jetbrains_marketplace_or_plugin
assertions.description: The agent mentions JetBrains Marketplace, a plugin ID, or an install archive.

assertions.name: sets_capability_expectation
assertions.description: The agent states whether highlighting and/or LSP is supported.

evals.name: birdcc-cli-install
evals.prompt: How do I install the birdcc CLI on macOS? I prefer not to install it globally if possible.
evals.expected_output: Offer npm install -g, npx @birdcc/cli, and local dev-dependency options. Include verification commands birdcc --version and a quick lint example.

assertions.name: mentions_npx_option
assertions.description: The agent offers npx / pnpm dlx / yarn dlx as a non-global option.

assertions.name: mentions_local_dev_dependency
assertions.description: The agent offers a local devDependency install option.

assertions.name: mentions_verification_command
assertions.description: The agent suggests verifying the installation with birdcc --version or similar.
